findings.message: The trace concludes that Bristol Central has 55 and East Catholic has 40, but the provided text only states that the labels and numbers appear on the scoreboard. It does not textually ground which score belongs to which team position, so the leader attribution and option mapping are not fully justified from the evidence text alone.

The trace says the 131.00s frame confirms end of the third quarter via text resembling "3rd" and "0:00.0", but the evidence summary also contains OCR from another interval showing "3rd" with a nonzero clock at 119.0s. The selected 131-132s interval is plausible, yet the answer-critical end-of-quarter confirmation relies on summarized OCR wording rather than a cleaner, unambiguous textual grounding of the exact scoreboard state.

feedback: The trace is close, but it overreaches in assigning the two scores to the two teams and thus in selecting option A. The text supports that the end-of-third-quarter scoreboard shows team labels and the numbers 55 and 40, but it does not explicitly ground which team has which score.
missing_information: Which displayed score is paired with Bristol Central and which is paired with East Catholic on the end-of-third-quarter scoreboard
A textually grounded confirmation that the selected scoreboard view is specifically the end of the third quarter
